I EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
I.1 Products covered by this document 
SEAICE_ARC_SEAICE_REP_OBSERVATIONS_011_010 
Sea ice drift maps are estimated in the Arctic from several scatterometers and radiometers. Single drift 
fields are combined at the same resolution into a “merged” field, built at two- three- six- and thirty-day 
lags (depending on the sensor(s) used), duri ng the winter seasons (September until May).   ASCAT1 and 
merged ASCAT/SSMI 2 ASCAT/QUIKSCAT3 and also CFOSAT  SCAT4 and merged CFOSAT SCAT/SSMI 
datasets are ~62.5 km (0.5 degree) spatial resolution while AMSR5 datasets are ~31.25 km (0.25 degree) 
spatial resolution. The CFOSAT data are time limited, covering the 2020-2022 period. 
SEAICE_ANT_PHY_L3_MY_011_018 
Sea ice drift maps are estimated in the Antarctic from AMSR radiometer datasets at ~31.25  km 
(0.25 degree) spatial resolution since 2003 at two - and three -day lags, during the winter seasons 
(October until April).  CFOSAT SCAT data are also used to build sea ice drift maps at two -, three- and 
thirty-day lags. CFOSAT SCAT and merged CFOSAT SCAT/SSMI datasets are ~62.5 km (0.5 degree) spatial 
resolution for the 2020-2022 period. 
I.2 Summary of the results 
I.2.1 Arctic sea ice drift 
SEAICE_ARC_SEAICE_REP_OBSERVATIONS_011_010 
The low resolution Arctic Sea ice drift products have been compared to buoy data (US Interagency Arctic 
Buoy Program (IABP6)). The validation shows good correlation between the reference and the product 
and standard deviation is 7.5  km for 3 -day lag drifts and 8.9 km for 6 -day lag drifts for both velocity 
components.  
In order to generate the drift maps, the same processing method is applied with the QUIKSCAT, SSMI, 
ASCAT and CFOSAT data.  
Be aware that the original CFOSAT data are still to be assessed by the space Chinese agency. 
The medium resolution Arctic Sea ice drift products have also been compared to buoy data (US IABP). 
The validation (see Table 1) shows good correlation between the reference and the product and 
 
1 Advanced Scatterometer sensors developed for the MetOp A, B and C missions.  
2 Special Sensor Microwave Imager instrument (passive microwave radiometer) developed for the Defense 
Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP). 
3 The NASA QuikSCAT (Quick Scatterometer) was an Earth observation satellite carrying the SeaWinds 
scatterometer. 
4 SCAT is a wind-scatterometer on board the Chinese-French Oceanic SATellite (CFOSAT) 
5 Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer on board Aqua and GCOM -W satellites. 
6 https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-arctic-buoy-program

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes Sea Ice TAC products for both Arctic and Antarctic regions, detailing data collection methods, validation frameworks, and results from buoy comparisons.
